entry.title: Re: Upload General Journal Entry - Incomplete documents
entry.content:
Hello again,

You're right sorry. I confused with the private cloud version.

In S4 hana cloud public, it's the "Upload General Journal Entries" app which is used instead.

Best regard,

Jeremy

entry.title: F5354 error when running KO88
entry.content:
Hi

I am getting error F5354 - Account "14000000" in company code "xxxx" is marked as a reconciliation account for account type "D" and cannot therefore be directly posted to.

I am using an transaction code KO88 - Internal Order Settlement. I am not using Account "14000000" in settlement rule.

Account 11254000 is not a Reconciliation Account

Either Sender Account (79003000) nor Receiver Account (11254000) is a reconciliation account, why sap is checking irrelevant account and giving error message?

Any solution is appreciated.

Hyperliquid

80% on Transaction

entry.title: Shift Factor Behavior When Only Early Completion Is Defined
entry.content:
Let’s say I have a strategy-based maintenance plan with the following settings:

  • Cycle = 10 days

  • Shift factor for early completion = 100%

  • Shift factor for late completion = (left blank)

  • Tolerance = 0% (so any variance will trigger a shift)

📅 The order was completed 5 days late.

Now I’m trying to understand how SAP handles the next planned date.

❓Question:

Since only the shift factor for early completion is maintained, and the order was completed late, will SAP apply the 100% early shift factor to this late completion?

If yes — that would mean:

  • Next plan date is shifted 5 days later (i.e., same effect as if late shift factor = 100%)

If no — then what fallback logic is applied in this case?

📘 What I Know So Far:

  • In IP11, I can define separate shift factors for early and late.

  • But some sources and instructor notes suggest that if only one shift factor is defined, SAP will apply that value for both early and late completions.

  • Others suggest late completions are only shifted if the late shift factor is explicitly defined.

I'm looking for confirmation from anyone who has tested this in a live system or encountered it in certification prep.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Need help understanding features per patch release
entry.content:
Dear SAP,

 

We are trying to understand how to get a comprehensive list of features that are included in each patch release of hybris DIGITS.  We are aware of the continuous innovation model.  We have been referencing this link to track each feature

However, it has come to our attention that this might not be all the features per patch.  We have recently learned of this link which is a What's New list for each releate.

I notice that the Whats New list contains many more features and changes per patch than the initial link above which seems to be only for features that have rollout phases.

So I'm trying to understand it.  Is it that the Whats New link shows all the new functionality for the patch release and only some of them will rollout in the phased approach?  What determines if a feature will just be part of a release or if it will be managed in a rolled out phase approach?  Please help me understand this clearly.  We are tasked with highlighted features in each patch release that our business users might find interesting or useful.  At the moment I feel that I need to use both of these links to get a full picture.  Is this the right approach or is there a better way to summarize and understand a patch release?

Thanks


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Need help understanding features per patch release
entry.content:
Hello!

Let me explain the difference between them:

Feature Rollout Phases
Starting with the 2211.4 release, new features are mostly backwards compatible, but some might change how your system works. These features are initially turned off to help ensure a smooth upgrade. In the future, they will be turned on by default and eventually become standard. It's a good idea to test and implement these features before they're automatically activated. This approach allows you to try out new features at your own pace and upgrade your systems with less risk.

So, taking as an example the first feature: "Configurable products: display discount messages and savings with rules," is deactivated by default in version 2211.4, but activated in 2211.12 (here you can choose to deactivate it or not), however this feature will be available out-of-the box in version 2211.20.

Now, If you go through the SAP link of the features per patch, in this same version 2211.20: Features Available in New Rollout Phases, the Display Discount Messages and Savings with Rules will de delivered by default.

The SAP link with the features per patch has the specific information like maintenance improvements, such as bug fixes, and new functionality.


Best regards,
Sabrina

entry.title: #SITCPS2025 - 🗣️ RISE with SAP Methodology
entry.content:
Sobre o Palestrante

Arthur Placido é um experiente consultor SAP, com mais de 17 anos de atuação nos módulos Sales and Distribution (SD) e Transportation Management (TM). Conhecido por seu olhar pragmático e foco funcional, Arthur é referência no setor por sua capacidade de traduzir conceitos metodológicos em práticas aplicáveis no campo.

Título da Palestra

Rise with SAP Methodology

Descrição da Palestra

Arthur detalha a metodologia "Rise with SAP", abordando as fases de onboarding, execução, ativação (go-live), ferramentas envolvidas e os elementos humanos do processo. Com exemplos claros e visão funcional, o conteúdo se dirige especialmente a consultores funcionais que desejam não apenas conhecer, mas aplicar efetivamente o modelo proposto pela SAP.

*** Link com todas as palestras ***

Resumo 

A Metodologia na Prática

A palestra começa com reflexões críticas sobre metodologias aplicadas em projetos SAP no Brasil, destacando problemas estruturais como comunicação ineficiente, ausência de franqueza, e foco em metas ilusórias. Arthur questiona práticas enraizadas e introduz a abordagem Rise como alternativa mais estruturada e adaptável.

Fundamentos do RISE with SAP

Arthur esclarece que "Rise" é antes de tudo um contrato comercial com estrutura metodológica baseada no "SAP Activate". Os pilares dessa metodologia são: padrões, ferramentas e pessoas.

  • Padrões: são diretrizes que organizam a entrega dos projetos dentro de boas práticas.

  • Ferramentas: como Signavio, SAP Form, BTP, Tricentis e SAP Build, apoiam a execução e a medição dos resultados.

  • Pessoas: o sucesso do projeto depende do preparo e do comprometimento dos times envolvidos.

Visão Funcional e Relevância para Consultores

Arthur defende que consultores funcionais precisam se envolver com as ferramentas tecnológicas, mesmo sem serem especialistas nelas. Ter noções de BTP, testes com Tricentis ou fluxos com Signavio pode ser determinante na fluidez do projeto.

Etapas e Componentes

A metodologia envolve fases claramente definidas com qualidade mensurável. O onboarding inicial, com um especialista da SAP, visa alinhar expectativas e apresentar o contrato. O Quality Gate, integrado ao SAP Form, audita cada etapa, e o uso de dashboards permite avaliar se as práticas do Clean Core estão sendo seguidas.

Clean Core como Paradigma

Clean Core não significa “zero desenvolvimento”, mas sim desenvolver com responsabilidade, mantendo o sistema limpo, escalável e reutilizável. Arthur enfatiza que é um princípio, e não um fim.

Aplicação e Realidade

A palestra conclui com o alerta de que, embora muitas ferramentas já estejam disponíveis, sua aplicabilidade depende da realidade de cada projeto. Consultores devem refletir sobre a utilidade e viabilidade de cada tecnologia no contexto do cliente.

se você deseja ser notificado sobre os próximos eventos SAP Inside Track no Brasil, entre na nossa lista de interesse: Cadastre-se aqui para ser notificado

entry.title: #SITCPS2025 - 🗣️Soluções SAP para Transformação de Negócios
entry.content:
Palestrante: Rodrigo Cordeiro @Rodrigo_Cordeiro 

Bio: Rodrigo Cordeiro é um profissional com mais de 25 anos de experiência em áreas como Planejamento Estratégico, Gestão de Logística e Gestão de Cadeia de Suprimentos. Atuando atualmente com transformação de negócios, ele alia visão prática e otimismo em sua abordagem.

Título da Palestra

Soluções SAP para Transformação de Negócios

Breve Descrição da Palestra

Nesta palestra, Rodrigo apresenta os desafios enfrentados pelas organizações diante da necessidade de constante adaptação. Com foco em soluções SAP como Signavio, LeanIX e WalkME, ele demonstra como essas ferramentas contribuem para otimizar processos de forma estratégica e eficaz, viabilizando uma adaptação ágil e sustentável.

*** Link com todas as palestras ***

A transformação como necessidade vital

Rodrigo inicia destacando que a transformação de negócios não é mais uma opção, mas uma questão de sobrevivência. Inspirado por reflexões de Darwin e Leon Megginson, ele ressalta que a adaptabilidade é a chave no atual cenário corporativo volátil e imprevisível.

A complexidade do cenário atual

Rodrigo ilustra como o ambiente de negócios se tornou mais complexo com a digitalização e a multiplicidade de ferramentas e processos. A SAP responde a isso com a estrutura de Business Transformation Management (BTM), que organiza a transformação com foco em pessoas, processos, dados e aplicações.

LeanIX: mapeando o caminho estratégico

Apresentado como um “mapa”, o LeanIX atua na camada estratégica da arquitetura corporativa. A ferramenta organiza os ativos de TI, identifica lacunas e alinha tecnologia à estratégia. Rodrigo alerta sobre o risco de usá-la apenas como catálogo e não como ferramenta de transformação.

Signavio: da linguagem comum à melhoria contínua

Tratado como um “facilitador de comunicação”, o Signavio atua na camada tática. Ele ajuda a traduzir os processos do negócio para a linguagem tecnológica e vice-versa, construindo uma ponte entre diferentes áreas. Rodrigo explica como suas ferramentas (como o Process Insights e Process Intelligence) trazem dados reais e objetivos, viabilizando melhorias baseadas em fatos e não em opiniões.

WalkME: personal trainer digital

Na camada operacional, WalkME surge como uma solução de adoção digital. Comparado a um personal trainer, ele acompanha o usuário em tempo real durante o uso de sistemas SAP, oferecendo orientação e treinamentos contextuais. Rodrigo ressalta o ganho de eficiência e aderência proporcionado pela ferramenta.

Integração entre soluções

Rodrigo também demonstra como essas soluções se integram — por exemplo, o mapeamento feito no Signavio revela lacunas que são preenchidas com dados do LeanIX. Esse ecossistema integrado torna o processo de transformação mais coerente e eficaz.

Reflexão final

Ele conclui com uma citação de Peter Drucker: “as únicas coisas que evoluem por vontade própria em uma organização são desordem, atrito e mau desempenho”. Para ele, a transformação exige intenção, estratégia e ferramentas adequadas.

se você deseja ser notificado sobre os próximos eventos SAP Inside Track no Brasil, entre na nossa lista de interesse: Cadastre-se aqui para ser notificado

entry.title: #SITCPS2025 - 🗣️Joule: A Nova Queridinha da SAP
entry.content:
Nome do Palestrante:
Luciano Casado

Breve Bio:
Luciano Casado é arquiteto de soluções e pré-vendas na GFX Consultoria, com mais de 15 anos de experiência em desenvolvimentos e integrações SAP. Formado em Sistemas de Informação, possui MBA em Arquitetura de Software e Soluções, além de ampla vivência técnica e de negócios no ecossistema SAP.

Título da Palestra:
Joule: A Nova Queridinha da SAP

Descrição da Palestra:
A apresentação tem como objetivo explicar o que é a Joule, como ela se conecta ao universo SAP e como pode facilitar a vida dos usuários de maneira prática e direta. Com exemplos ao vivo, demonstrações técnicas e uma visão estratégica, Luciano revela o papel da Joule como assistente digital integrada às soluções SAP.

*** Link com todas as palestras ***

Apresentação e Motivação Pessoal:
Luciano inicia com uma introdução pessoal e descontraída, ressaltando sua trajetória, paixão por tecnologia e a importância da comunidade técnica para compartilhar conhecimento.

Contextualização da IA no Ambiente Corporativo:
A inteligência artificial (IA) não é mais futurismo, mas realidade no mundo corporativo. Segundo dados apresentados, mais de 72% das empresas já utilizam IA de alguma forma em 2024.

Conceito de Business AI da SAP:
A SAP introduziu o conceito de "Business AI", que se baseia em dados de negócio provenientes de suas soluções transacionais (como S/4HANA, Ariba, CX, SuccessFactors). Essa camada intermediária utiliza IA de grandes provedores (como OpenAI e Google) para oferecer valor ao cliente final, mantendo segurança de dados e foco exclusivo em contextos corporativos.

Quem é Joule:
Joule é a assistente digital (copiloto) da SAP, comparada a uma “Alexa corporativa”. Sua função é atuar dentro de cada contexto SAP (como S/4HANA, CX, etc.), trazendo agilidade e praticidade para usuários, consultores e clientes finais, com uma interface amigável e em constante evolução.

Demonstrações Práticas:
Luciano realiza várias simulações com a Joule no SAP S/4HANA Public Edition. Exemplos incluem:

  • Listagem de ordens de compra não liberadas.

  • Identificação de pedidos de compra atrasados.

  • Consulta de pedidos de venda não entregues e geração de remessas.

Em todos os casos, Joule executa as ações diretamente ou direciona o usuário aos aplicativos SAP corretos, com dados previamente filtrados.

Para Consultores e Clientes:
Consultores ganham produtividade e autonomia com Joule, que poderá futuramente consultar toda a documentação SAP e indicar soluções de configuração. Clientes terão mais agilidade para acessar informações sem intermediação da TI.

Para Desenvolvedores:
Luciano demonstra a integração da Joule com o SAP Build para desenvolvimento rápido de aplicações. Joule pode gerar scripts e lógica de programação com base em comandos em linguagem natural, acelerando o ciclo de desenvolvimento.

Futuro da Joule:
SAP planeja expandir as capacidades da Joule com os conceitos de:

  • Julie Agents: múltiplos agentes especializados que colaboram entre si.

  • Julie Studio: ambiente para customizar e monetizar funcionalidades da Joule.

  • Julie for ABAP: sugestões e geração de código ABAP automatizado.

Conclusão:
A Joule representa um novo paradigma de interação com o SAP. Vai além de uma simples interface, funcionando como mediadora inteligente entre dados, processos e pessoas. É uma transformação que impactará diretamente a produtividade, a experiência do usuário e o valor entregue pelas soluções SAP.

 

se você deseja ser notificado sobre os próximos eventos SAP Inside Track no Brasil, entre na nossa lista de interesse: Cadastre-se aqui para ser notificado

entry.title: Bill of Exchange - clearing account is missing in table T045W
entry.content:
hello everyone,

I have been trying for days to set up the bill of exchange payment method in public cloud. I have made all the settings I can think of, but the posting of the payment run always stops at the same point. 

However, I simply cannot find a point in the customising where I can set the linking of the clearing account. (former OBYK i think)

When posting the payment, the system runs into the following error, see Message attached:

entry.title: #SITCPS2025 - 🗣️ Como Extrair o Melhor do SAP com SAP Fiori e Personalizações Clean Core
entry.content:
Nome do Palestrante

Lucas Ferreira

Breve Biografia

Lucas Ferreira é Executivo Sênior na Tachyonix, com 11 anos de experiência na SAP Brasil. Especialista em pré-vendas, S4 e BTP, ele se dedica a soluções que promovem inovação e eficiência nos processos empresariais.

Título da Palestra

Como Extrair o Melhor do SAP com SAP Fiori e Personalizações Clean Core

Descrição Breve sobre a Palestra

Nesta palestra, Lucas Ferreira demonstrou como maximizar o potencial do SAP utilizando o SAP Fiori e personalizações Clean Core. Ele apresentou métodos para criar aplicações Fiori sem a necessidade de programação, mantendo a integridade das funcionalidades padrão e proporcionando uma experiência mais ágil e eficiente para os usuários.

*** Link com todas as palestras ***

Resumo

  • Introdução ao SAP Fiori e Clean Core: A palestra iniciou com uma explicação sobre o SAP Fiori, destacando sua interface amigável e adaptável, e o conceito de Clean Core, que visa manter o núcleo do sistema SAP limpo de personalizações invasivas.

  • Benefícios das Personalizações Clean Core: Lucas enfatizou que as personalizações Clean Core permitem adaptações às necessidades específicas das empresas sem comprometer as atualizações futuras do sistema, garantindo maior estabilidade e menor custo de manutenção.

  • Criação de Aplicações Fiori sem Programação: Foi demonstrado como é possível desenvolver aplicações Fiori utilizando ferramentas de low-code/no-code, facilitando a criação de soluções personalizadas por usuários com pouco ou nenhum conhecimento em programação.

  • Integração com SAP BTP: A integração com a SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) foi abordada, mostrando como ela suporta as extensões Clean Core e permite a criação de aplicações escaláveis e seguras.

  • Casos Práticos e Resultados: Lucas compartilhou casos de sucesso onde a adoção do SAP Fiori e das personalizações Clean Core resultaram em melhorias significativas na eficiência operacional e na satisfação dos usuários finais.

  • Recomendações Finais: A palestra concluiu com recomendações para as empresas que desejam adotar o SAP Fiori e as personalizações Clean Core, enfatizando a importância de uma estratégia bem definida e do envolvimento das equipes de TI e de negócios.

se você deseja ser notificado sobre os próximos eventos SAP Inside Track no Brasil, entre na nossa lista de interesse: Cadastre-se aqui para ser notificado

entry.title: Re: Steps to configure Custom SMTP Server on SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content: Thank you @varun_gupta15 for very detailed explanation about the configuration of Email server configuration.

entry.title: #SITCPS2025 - 🗣️ SAP Business AI: Aplicação de IA Generativa em Cenários de Negócio
entry.content:
Palestrante:
Sávio Marques

Biografia:
Profissional com mais de 20 anos de experiência em TI, especializado em soluções SAP. Atua como BTP Solution Advisor na SAP, com foco em pré-vendas, adoção e geração de demanda. É especialista em SAP Build Suite, CAP, Fiori e Integration Suite, com profundo conhecimento em aplicações de IA Generativa.

Título da Palestra:
SAP Business AI: Aplicação de IA Generativa em Cenários de Negócio

Descrição da Palestra:
Sávio Marques apresentou como a SAP está incorporando IA Generativa em suas soluções empresariais. Ele discutiu o conceito de RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) e o impacto estratégico da IA nos processos de negócio, destacando agentes inteligentes, integração com LLMs e a democratização da tecnologia.

*** Link com todas as palestras ***

Resumo Educativo

  • Conceito de SAP Business AI:
    A SAP está reformulando suas soluções para aplicar inteligência artificial de forma prática e estratégica, criando a linha "SAP Business AI" que visa integrar IA diretamente nos processos empresariais.

  • Importância dos Dados Harmonizados:
    Sávio destaca que a base da aplicação de IA está na organização dos dados transacionais. Quanto mais padronizados e enriquecidos semanticamente, melhor o desempenho das ferramentas de IA para gerar insights.

  • Agentes Inteligentes e a Revolução no SAP:
    A palestra mostra como os agentes com IA generativa estão começando a executar tarefas complexas autonomamente, como consultas de pedidos, criação de ordens de venda e interação com usuários em linguagem natural.

  • Parceria com Modelos de Linguagem (LLMs):
    A SAP firmou contratos com provedores como OpenAI, Gemini e Mistral, assegurando que os dados do cliente não serão usados para treinar os modelos, mantendo a conformidade com LGPD.

  • Integração com SAP BTP:
    O BTP é a base onde são desenvolvidas soluções personalizadas com IA. Inclui serviços de extração de dados de documentos fiscais usando IA, mesmo sem padrões definidos, e integração com ferramentas como Databricks.

  • Exemplos Práticos de Aplicações:
    Foram demonstradas aplicações de IA em SAP S/4HANA, como previsão de atraso em pedidos, compensação bancária e reconciliação de estoque. Muitas delas já estão disponíveis desde versões de 2020.

  • Orquestração e Criação de Agentes:
    Mostrou-se como criar agentes que utilizam LLMs, conectam-se com dados do SAP e executam fluxos completos de processos de forma automatizada, como criar ordens de venda com campos definidos dinamicamente.

  • Uso de Documentos Vetorizados e RAG:
    O sistema também é capaz de utilizar vetorização de documentos para fornecer respostas precisas em contexto, como recomendar palestras com base em documentos do evento.

  • Reflexão Final:
    Sávio encerra incentivando os participantes a refletirem sobre quais tarefas repetitivas em seus trabalhos podem ser otimizadas com IA, democratizando o acesso a tecnologias de ponta.

se você deseja ser notificado sobre os próximos eventos SAP Inside Track no Brasil, entre na nossa lista de interesse: Cadastre-se aqui para ser notificado

entry.title: Palestras realizadas #SITCPS2025
entry.content:
Propósito do Evento (#SITCPS2025) 

O SAP Inside Track é um evento comunitário global, organizado por membros da comunidade SAP, com o objetivo de promover o aprendizado colaborativo, networking e a troca de experiências sobre soluções SAP. Em Campinas, o evento destacou-se como uma oportunidade única para os participantes se atualizarem sobre as tendências tecnológicas e práticas recomendadas no universo SAP. Veja o blog sobre o evento.

Destaques das Palestras

O evento contou com uma programação diversificada de palestras, abordando temas como inteligência artificial, personalizações no SAP Fiori, metodologias de transformação digital, entre outros. Especialistas compartilharam insights valiosos e casos práticos, proporcionando aos participantes uma visão abrangente das possibilidades e desafios no uso das soluções SAP.

Palestra Nome do Palestrante Link

Inteligência Artificial para Consultores Funcionais@Secco Link
O fim da TAXBRARVABRA e a chegada do TaaS@fabiodezan Link
Uso de APIs na Comunicação Bancária dos Processos Financeiros no SAP@MRobalinho Link
RISE with SAP MethodologyArthur PlacidoLink
Joule: A Nova Queridinha da SAPLuciano CasadoLink
Soluções SAP para Transformação de Negócios (Signavio e LeanIX)@Rodrigo_Cordeiro Link
Como Extrair o Melhor do SAP com SAP Fiori e Personalizações Clean CoreLucas FerreiraLink
SAP Business AI: Aplicação de IA Generativa em Cenários de NegócioSávio MarquesLink
Desafios da Reforma Tributária Brasileira@thamires_k + PudenziLink

se você deseja ser notificado sobre os próximos eventos SAP Inside Track no Brasil, entre na nossa lista de interesse: Cadastre-se aqui para ser notificado

entry.title: Re: I want to migrate the configuration from NAST to BRF+ between S/4HANA and Commerce Automation.

2) Please read Asking Good Questions in the SCN Discussion Spaces... - SAP Community
3) This is not an ABAP question so why tagging ABAP?
4) It's not clear which configuration you mean
5) Documentation can be found at help.sap.com

entry.title: Re: $search not working for external service
entry.content: Yes, the issue was that search functionality wasnt implemented properly in the backend system( S/4HANA ). Once implemented, there was no need to set the where clause in code

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance for Perú - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition DemoStarter
entry.content:
Hello Experts

Currently, the SAP DRC solution is active and the legal reports are working. However , I am unable to create new custom reports in the “Define Statutory Reports” app.

My user has all the required roles.

What could be missing?
Is another configuration missing?

#Sap Document and Reporting Compliance

#SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Best regards,

Antony Pantoja

required data does not exist in this plant IAI1 for this material

entry.title: How do I create a service with SAP PO and NetWeaver using a WSDL from a SOAP 1.2 service?
entry.content:
Hello everyone. I am trying to create a service via SPROXY in my SAP system using a WSDL that is SOAP 1.2. The problem is that no matter how much documentation or guides I look for, I can't get a result. I configured my service this way:


Sender Channel

Receiver Channel

I am performing the test by executing my proxy from SPROXY, calling from the outbound system interface, which in this case is the SAP system, to the SOAP 1.2 service, which is the entity that owns the WSDL.

However, the most persistent error in this case is the following: SOAP: Response message contains an errorXIAdapter/HTTP/ADAPTER.HTTP_EXCEPTION - HTTP 415 Cannot process the message because the content type application/xml was not the expected type application/soap+xml; charset=utf-8.

It's worth adding that the WSDL has certificate authentication (which I possess), but I haven't put it in the receiver channel image yet because I wanted to solve this problem first and then configure the credentials.

I have tried many things to solve this problem:

  1. Creating an XSL mapping for both the request and the response to generate a SOAP 1.2 that would be accepted, but it didn't work (the guide I based this on was the following: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/soap-1-2-protocol-connection-issue-in-sap-po7-5/qaq-p/12331541).

  2. Using HTTP (axis) because it has the SOAP 1.2 option, but when I use that, the error changes to NullPointerException. From here, I investigated and it seems I don't have the axis framework in my SAP PO (NetWeaver 7.50). I tried to install the framework on my own, but I couldn't find a detailed guide to do so, so I couldn't manage it.

  3. Trying other methods of creating this service, but I don't have access to SAP Cloud Process Integration (CPI) or the SOAMANAGER transaction to create an RFC function module. I also tried creating my own service in SICF, but that didn't work either.

Your help would be very useful, especially if it's a step-by-step guide or things to keep in mind. If you need more information, I can gladly provide it, although the only thing I cannot show is the WSDL, as it is my personal information.

As additional information, in ESR, I created data and message types to do the corresponding mapping to the External View, where the outbound sends the request message type to the request external view of the inbound for its mapping, and in turn, this returns the response from the external view to the response message type for the mapping.

I'll be waiting for your response and thank you very much in advance.

entry.title: Re: Troubleshooting ‘Reference Lost’ error in Fiori Launchpad Designer
entry.content:
I have the same problem as sanguinETHi2402

¿What would be the solution?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: 1. Does the application containing any 3rd party libr...
entry.content:
Log4j is Java only.

Need more details about what you are using?

entry.title: sm36 smartforms report not working
entry.content:
I have a question for everyone: I have a report that uses SmartForm to print documents. The front-end execution is fine and everything is normal. However, when using SM36 to schedule the execution of this report in the backend, the running result is directly in a cancelled state without any error messages. What should I do?

During normal front-end execution, there were no issues, the operation was successful, and the documents could be printed：

When executing SM36 in the backend, there are no errors reported, and the status is directly set to cancelled.
sm37 Job overview:


Job log:No log entries are available for the selected job.

SM36:YXSPRINTDEMO

entry.title: VAS not automatically generated in FMSO - MSO scenario
entry.content:
I am creating a Multi ship to order scenario.
I am creating FMSO and when I am checking for VAS item attached I do not see any data.
I have assigned vas to my main material using FSH_vas_sd and ideally it should directly be attached in the FMSO.
Currently I have to add manually.
Is there any customisation missing?

entry.title: Re: Create Outbound Delivery in EWM: 2025 Edition
entry.content:
it may be at some cases easier to use /SCWM/CL_DLV_MANAGEMENT_PRD( )->CREATE_WHR( )

even it it does not provide full functionality it can be start point to create delivery and you can adjust it afterwards with /scdl/cl_dl_header_prd and /scdl/cl_dl_item_prd .

I am not sure regarding message texts, do you mean to see it directly in debugger? I do not have test report which I can debug to check, but the CREATE_WHR you can see as well how to get messages

      lt_messages = lo_message_box->get_messages( iv_doccat = ls_a_head-doccat iv_docid = ls_a_head-docid ).
      DELETE lt_messages WHERE docid IS INITIAL.
      IF NOT lt_messages IS INITIAL.
        CREATE OBJECT lo_message
          EXPORTING
            it_message = lt_messages.
        eo_message->add( lo_message ).
      ENDIF.

and after you get these with eo_message->get_messages( ) as /SCDL/DM_MESSAGE_TAB where it is basically syst fields for message.

I cannot say delivery creation in EWM is such a rare case in the past year I had it on two projects (if only ODO are taken into account) . But good thing is, since in EWM all requests follow same logic now it is not a problem to create as well other document categories like SPC or WMR.

entry.title: Re: Problem Triggering Start of Selection
entry.content: Thanks for "change its value to ONLI". You saved my day!

entry.title: Re: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance for Perú - SAP...
entry.content:
Hello again dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

The reason you’re not seeing the “Create” button in the Define Statutory Reports app is likely one of these:

1. Missing authorization

You probably don’t have the right role. You need the business catalog:
SAP_CA_DRC_REPORT_CONF_PC (Statutory Reporting - Configuration)
Without it, you can only view reports, not create them.

 

2. Demo or restricted tenant

If you're working in a Demo Starter System, the ability to create reports may be intentionally disabled. Try again in your test (Q) or production system.

 

3. UI glitch

Sometimes it’s just a display issue. Try refreshing the page, clearing your cache, or switching browsers but in your case, I think this reason is not the one honestly.

 

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance for Perú - SAP...
entry.content: Not a blog here, it's Q&A or Discussions or whatever else.

entry.title: Re: sm36 smartforms report not working
entry.content: Look at the job log, it explains why the job has been cancelled.

entry.title: Re: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance for Perú - SAP...
entry.content:
Hello @Antony_Pantoja 

Thank you for your question

Likely Causes and Solutions:

Missing Write Access to Configuration Environment

Even with all roles assigned, the “Define Statutory Reports” app only allows report creation in a configuration tenant (CBC or Q system)—not in production systems.

Check:
Are you in a Starter or Production system? If so, the button will not appear.

Solution:
You must be in a Customer Configuration Tenant (Q system) to create or edit report definitions. This is by SAP design.

Role vs. Business Catalog Check

Ensure that you not only have the correct roles but also the business catalogs required for edit access:

Required Catalogs for Report Creation:

  • SAP_CA_BC_DRC_REP_PC (DRC Report Processing)
  • SAP_CA_BC_DRC_REP_CONF_PC (DRC Report Configuration)

Solution:
Ask your administrator to confirm that your role includes both catalogs with write access.

Configuration Mode, Missing in CBC

If you are working via SAP Central Business Configuration (CBC), make sure the activity "Define Statutory Reports" is enabled for configuration in the corresponding CBC scope.

App Variant Restriction

Ensure you're using the correct version of the app. Some customers mistakenly use a read-only variant (typically used in productive tenants or for viewing standard content).

What you can check:

  • Confirm you're working in a customer configuration system (Q system), not starter or production
  •  Verify your user has write-access catalogs (SAP_CA_BC_DRC_REP_CONF_PC)
  • Ensure CBC scope includes “Define Statutory Reports”
  • Check with your system admin if a custom UI adaptation or launchpad restriction was applied

Let me know if you need further support

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Can't copy existing app
entry.content:
Dear Pekka @agpekka ,

The copy app is now functional, thank you. 

We are still missing the app renaming function.

Concerning the file loading when creating new app, could you please upload the threshold of file size to 400 or 500MB?

Thank you for your attention

Best regards

Chantal

entry.title: Re: Bill of Exchange - clearing account is missing in tab...
entry.content:
Hello 

The Bill of Exchange is partially supported, but only for a few countries and scenarios.

  • SAP’s Multi-Tenant Public Cloud does not expose the full range of BoE functionalities that are available in  Private Cloud, such as:
    • BoE acceptance
    • Discounting
    • Protest handling
    • Maturity & liability account tracking
  • You will not find configuration options like OBYK or FBWP (BoE payment program settings),

You can configure via SSCUI apps

Table summary

Requirement

Public Cloud Status

BoE full process (OBYK, FBWP, discounting)

 Not available in Public Cloud

BoE payment method definition

 Possible, but without full posting logic

Clearing account config (OBYK)

 Not available

Workaround

 Use Down Payments + Manual Postings

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Custom events trigger from S/4 Hana to SAP BTP Event Mesh
entry.content:
Hi Pratik Ingawale,

I've given a hard coded values in FM which is being shown in payload, but the container values are empty. why is it empty? Kindly help!

Thanks,

Bramhani G

entry.title: Re: Landed Cost Management in SAP S/4 HANA Public cloud
entry.content:
Hello @FKai 

To manage landed costs in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud using Moving Average Price:

 Use the “Manage Landed Costs” app to allocate non-merchandise costs post-GR, ensuring accurate inventory valuation and MAP adjustment.

This standard approach is recommended and fully integrated with the Public Cloud best practices.

Handling Landed Costs in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (MAP Method) – Summary

  • Use the “Manage Landed Costs” app to allocate additional charges (freight, customs, etc.) after goods receipt.
  • Reference the original Inbound Delivery or Purchase Order in the Landed Cost Document.
  • Allocate costs based on quantity, value, or weight.
  • The system automatically adjusts the Moving Average Price and updates the inventory value.
  • Record related invoices (non-PO based) using “Create Supplier Invoice” referencing the Landed Cost Document.

Thank you and kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Meetup Riga - May 29, 2025
entry.content: Can't miss it. See you there 🤣

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP ABAP Environment – Pre-Upgrade Option for Release 2505
entry.content: Yes, the upgrade runs as planned

entry.title: Re: sm36 smartforms report not working
entry.content: No log. No log entries are available for the selected job.

entry.title: Re: Bill of Exchange - clearing account is missing in table T045W
entry.content: Please indicate the message number for finding the question (and solution hopefully): message F5068 "A clearing account is missing in table T045W for account 11007865"

entry.title: Re: Bill of Exchange - clearing account is missing in tab...
entry.content: The message is F5068, not L5068.

entry.title: Re: Data Purging Process in BW4HANA
entry.content:
Hi Shyamala

The manual manipulation of the data in BW Infoprovider tables is not supported. So having a program which practically does simply a DELETE FROM is problematic.
The official way of removing data selectively based on a time parameter from ADSO-s is one of these or their combination:

  • Selective deletion (requires a date field in the ADSO which can be used to delete the data)
  • Request Cleanup
    • Takes the date of the loading request
    • Can be used only for certain types of ADSO-s (clean up CL table or AQ table of inbound only ADSO or activate requests in cube like ADSO)
  • In case you also have COLD store data to be deleted:
    • https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BW4HANA/107a6e8a38b74ede94c833ca3b7b6f51/adece7bde084468dbb0d20fa3b2cdd1b.html?locale=en-US
    • https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BW4HANA/107a6e8a38b74ede94c833ca3b7b6f51/b912a621e66c4da3a0a577da178bad47.html?locale=en-US

Also check:

3232938 [BW Central KBA] How-To: Data Management for BW Infoprovider tables

 

br

Roland

entry.title: Re: sm36 smartforms report not working
entry.content: Okay, I should learn reading the questions. Can other jobs output a job log? i.e. is there a general job and job log problem?

entry.title: Re: ModuleError: failed to load 'unit/unitTests.qunit.js'
entry.content: i am getting the same error can you help please

entry.title: Re: Launch SAP BW process chain from Fiori
entry.content:
You are right. There are many statements which cannot be used in RAP. You might try to call a wrapper function which starts this process in a background process. 

Also please format your code using the editor available.

entry.title: Re: Internal Order Budgeting
entry.content:
Ideally the technical clearing account is balance sheet account have you activated record account assignment for this GL it should not be possible, system allow CE category 90 or record account assignment for GL maintained in AO90, check with SAP they should be able to provide correction note.

Also check note 2274763

entry.title: Re: eDocument not not relevant"
entry.content:
The eDocument creation from billing documents is automatic and managed via a settings within FIBF transaction in the OP systems. So ideally they should appear instantly as the regular billing documents get posted and the accounting entries are released. Topic E28 from KBA 2530509 - Electronic Document Processing Framework Troubleshooting Guide - SAP for Me, should give you some idea.

The main problem may, eDocument appearing as 'Not Relevant', may occur due to certain missing customization, which country solution are you using? Every country solution will have certain shared customizations(from the eDocument Framework) and some related to the country solution itself. Here's an example of Greece eDocument solution Customizing | SAP Help Portal. Have you checked for the similar customization option for your country specific solution?

entry.title: Re: BSP future in Private Cloud 2023 FPS2
entry.content:
Thanks for getting back to me, yes we are visiting old school UI technology, and I recall being trained on BSPs the back in 2004 (still got the SAP manuals) back when SDN was the place to go (guess most content was lost).

Yes just to clarify that these are legacy classic custom BSP apps (mixture of HMTL and ABAP), so yes definitely not SAPUI5, although as you correctly state they both show in SE80 as BSP apps.  The apps date from circa 2007.

They appear to still be functional in S/4HANA 2023 and Work Zone, despite the fact as you state this is considered old UI technology and no-one in their right mind today would consider using BSPs.

I assume the correct way forward is to refactor these to Fiori Freestyle using current Fiori design guidelines.  The fact this has not happened in the current ECC system prior to now is something of an oversight and before my time at this organisation, but I 'd personally be inclined to refactor as part of S/4HANA transformation activities (WRICEF/TOSL), and on a modern stack, ideally building on the BTP stack, rather than attempt to refactor to SAPUI5 on an ECC system where UI5 libraries, versions and theming are not current.  Deploying BTP to ECC stack is not currently an option either.  

I suppose the question is if these still run from FLP in S/4HANA private, like WDA or classic GUI reports and transaction also, will SAP at some point stop these legacy UIs from working.  I've also been searching for something that clearly expresses that this legacy UI technology is not supported in S/4HANA, but to no avail and my experience suggests that if technology is no longer supported, it broadly continues to function in S/4HANA. I guess given the level of organisations still running these older UI technologies on-prem or in private cloud, and not all legacy standard transactions have suitable Fiori alternatives, that would require an advance communication exercise that should be fairly easy to locate.

Thanks, Steve

entry.title: Re: How to customize tax codes and depreciation rules in...
entry.content:
Hello @AlbertFeng 

Below is a summarized Tax code and depreciation rules

Requirement

Recommended Approach

Special Depreciation Methods

Create custom depreciation keys using SSCUI (Manage Your Solution)

Set up additional depreciation areas (e.g., for tax vs IFRS reporting)

 

Rwandan Tax-Specific Adjustments

Use manual journal entries or custom fields to capture tax treatment.

 Use BAdIs or key user extensibility if automated tax calculation is needed.

 

Reporting & Compliance

Use standard reports (e.g., Asset History Sheet) or custom CDS views

Export to Excel or SAP Analytics Cloud for tax authority formats

 

System Limitations & Compliance

stay within Public Cloud restrictions (no backend ABAP mods, only approved logic)

This reflects SAP’s official extensibility strategy for Public Cloud. If Rwanda’s depreciation rules require functionality not available via SSCUIs or extensibility, you may need to:

  • Raise a feature request via SAP Influence Portal, or
  • Consider SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud, which allows full customization

Let me know it helps

With regards

Chuma

entry.title: I can't find a Depreciation key for qatar region that calculated depreciation days based.
entry.content: I can't find a Depreciation key for the Qatar region that calculates depreciation days based on. Is there any way to do it?

entry.title: Optimizing Financial Services with AI: A Seamless Invoice Inquiry Resolution Journey
entry.content:
In the dynamic world of finance operations, managing supplier communications efficiently is crucial to maintaining trust and transparency. With the integration of AI-powered tools, finance teams are now better equipped to handle invoice inquiries with speed and precision. Here's how AI transforms a typical overdue invoice inquiry into a seamless, intelligent support experience.

🔑Key Capabilities

Here are the core capabilities enabled by Generative AI in SAP Enterprise Service Management:

Capability Description
Business Information ExtractionExtracts structured data (like Invoice ID, Order Number) from unstructured inputs such as email subject and body.
Document ExtractionParses structured data from PDF/image attachments (e.g., invoices, checks) using AI-trained schemas.
Document ClassificationAutomatically categorizes documents based on predefined classes (e.g., Invoice, PO, Receipt).
Auto-Executable ExtractionAutomatically extracts and populates fields in the business document tab without agent intervention.
Semantic Business Document TypesMaps extracted data to meaningful backend business entities (Invoice, Order, Request).
Integration with SAP S/4HANAEnables real-time lookup, validation, and posting of documents to SAP S/4HANA and other systems.
Prompt-Based AI GuidanceCustom scenario and schema prompts to enhance extraction precision.

✅Prerequisites

Before implementing these capabilities, ensure the following components and configurations are in place:

Technical Prerequisites

Requirement Description
SAP Service Cloud V2 / SAP Enterprise Service ManagementEnsure the latest release is in use.
SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)For workflow, AI core models, and integration runtime.
SAP Integration Suite (CPI)To connect SAP Service Cloud V2 with SAP S/4HANA or external systems.

Functional Prerequisites

  • Enabled Case Management module.

  • Defined Case Types and Business Document Types.

  • Prepared Sample Documents (emails, invoices) for schema/scenario training.

🧠 Architecture Overview

 

📩Invoice Inquiry Email Initiation

It all begins with a supplier noticing an overdue invoice. Seeking clarification, the supplier sends an email to the finance team, including the invoice number and an attachment of the original invoice document. This initiates a service journey that blends human expertise with AI-driven support.

 

🚨Real-Time Access Notification to Open a Case

As soon as the email is received, the Finance Support Agent gets an automated alert indicating a new case assignment. This real-time notification ensures that no inquiry falls through the cracks and that agents can respond promptly, improving overall service responsiveness.

 

🗂️ Verifying and Categorizing the Case

The Finance Support Agent accesses the case, reviewing essential metadata such as:

  • Subject
  • Case Type
  • Supplier Name
  • Status
  • Priority

Thanks to intelligent workflows, even newly onboarded agents can consistently classify and prioritize cases, laying the groundwork for a standardized and scalable support process.

🧠 AI-Generated Case Summary

To reduce manual effort and ensure nothing is missed, an AI engine generates a concise summary of the case. This summary consolidates all relevant data points—invoice number, supplier, query type, and more—helping the agent quickly understand the context and make faster decisions.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

🔍AI-Driven Invoice Data Extraction

The agent leverages AI-based data extraction tools to identify and analyze the details within the attached invoice. These technologies reduce the need for manual data entry and ensure accuracy when verifying key information such as:

  • Invoice number
  • Amount
  • Due date
  • Payment status

 

✉️AI-Powered Email Drafting

Using an AI email assistant, the Finance Support Agent crafts a clear, professional response. The email outlines the blocked status, explains the missing items, and requests the delivery of the remaining widgets. It also confirms that payment will be processed upon receipt of the full order.

✅Resolution and Case Closure

Once the supplier receives the response and acknowledges the resolution path, the agent updates the case status to "Completed." The entire process—from inquiry to resolution—demonstrates the power of AI-enhanced workflows in accelerating and improving financial support services.

💡Key Takeaways

  • Real-time alerts drive faster case initiation.
  • AI-generated summaries speed up decision-making.
  • Data extraction tools ensure accurate invoice validation.
  • AI email drafting simplifies professional communication.
  • Standardized workflows ensure consistent case handling.

🚀The Future of Finance Support with SAP

This scenario showcases how SAP solutions integrated with AI capabilities can optimize finance support operations. By automating repetitive tasks, offering intelligent insights, and streamlining communications, finance teams can deliver exceptional supplier experiences—all while maintaining operational efficiency.

Settings for AI scenarios

Generative AI Scenarios --> Business Information Extraction

Step 1 – Define Input Sources
Identify if the case input will come from subject, description, or attachments. Choose based on where relevant information (e.g., invoice number) is typically found.

Step 2 – Configure a Business Information Extraction Scenario
If using subject/description, define a scenario and extraction elements (like Invoice ID). This guides the language model to extract info from free text.

 

Step 3 – Configure a Document Extraction Schema
If attachments are used, create a schema with header fields and descriptions. This enables structured extraction from documents like checks or invoices.

 

Step 4 – Create a Business Document Type
Define a document type with relevant columns (e.g., Invoice ID, Status). Columns pull data from extraction or backend API and allow manual entry if needed.

entry.title: I want to understand how cost travel from sales order, purchase order;
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I want to understand how the cost travels from the sales order to the purchase order. Are there any resources that you can share with me.

entry.title: Re: Requirement to Display Two Sales Org Addresses on MSDS
entry.content:
Hello

thanks for more infos..

Coming back to:

To display a Sales Org address, we rely on a custom table that maintains a 1:1 Country-to-Sales Org mapping. This has worked well so far, as each country typically has a centralized headquarters whose address can be displayed as the primary one.

However, Chile poses an exception. There is no centralized HQ; instead, smaller countries like Chile operate via distributed centers. Using the address of CL1 when the shipment originates from CL2 has already led to customer complaints due to inaccurate SDS address information.

i still have some doubt if i have understood your "landscape" correct.

My logic run like: you can define your sales organisations based on needs. You can define (if needed) two sales organisations located in Chile.

So my "simple" story would be: the real sales is done by sales org 1 or sales org 2. The SAP Standard chapter one story exactly support it like that.

Reading between the line: you have prepared a "custom driven country <> Address logic)

Which is any how customer driven.

So why not simply extend your  logic using "country <> sales org <> address". The real sales proess is any how related to a sales org; As we clearly talk about a parameter symbol: no change (assumption) needed in WWI template. In doing so you have a clear address determintion.

But yes: if you would try to go back now using only SAP standard symbol: you must "regenerated" your released documents.

Most of the companies i have a talk with are not using SAP standard for Chapter 1.

Wish you the best

C.B.

entry.title: Re: Billing Document Requests number range
entry.content:
Hello ISoma,

As a configuration expert, you can maintain number range intervals by performing the Define Number Ranges for Billing Documents configuration activity. Find this activity by using the search function in your configuration environment. For more information, see Configuration Environment of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Morę information You can find in this link https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/0df51b26f0514d0082b5fb8ff6ff99cb.html

Best regards, Łukasz

entry.title: Re: Mass SPRO Special Procurement Key config options
entry.content:
Hi Jeff008, 

my recommendation is to open SE16N in editable mode. Filter result for specific key for table T460T. Update data (best way is to export to Excel file, update there and copy paste to se16n). Next add this table to transport order to pass data to other environments. 

Best regards, Łukasz

entry.title: Re: Calling Custom Adobe form in ABAP On Cloud (Embedded Steampunk)
entry.content:
Hi,
 
I have a problem with this part of the code. Copy the class from git.
When executing this method:
DATA(ls_template) = lo_store->get_template_by_name( iv_get_binary = abap_false
iv_form_name = 'xxxx' 
iv_template_name = 'yyyy' ).
 
 
After debugging, I verified that in the "get_template_by_name" method, 
it is not assigning the value to the "lr_data" field.
the "lv_json_response" field is filled in
 
 
DATA(lv_json_response) = lo_response->get_text( ).
DATA lr_data TYPE REF TO data.
lr_data = /ui2/cl_json=>generate(
  json = lv_json_response
  pretty_name = /ui2/cl_json=>pretty_mode-camel_case
).
 
 
Any tips?

entry.title: Re: Standard Localization - Organizational Structure
entry.content:
Hi talke,

there is no list for this. Predefined organizational unit are install during installing Best Practices for specific counties.

Best regards, Łukasz

entry.title: Re: How sorting works in an internal table in given case??
entry.content:
Dear ABAPer_1631,

When you execute this sort command SORT lt_result BY field1 ASCENDING field2 DESCENDING you'll get a follow Table:

Primary key: field1 (ASCENDING)
- All rows have field1 = 12345
- Since every value is identical, the order remains unchanged

Secondary key: field2 (DESCENDING)
- Date fields (TYPE D) are stored internally as an integer in the format YYYYMMDD
- With DESCENDING, the comparison is lexicographic/numeric 'backwards' (newer dates (larger numbers) come before older dates (smaller numbers)).

Stable sort for identical keys
- If two records share the same values in field1 and field2, their original insertion order is preserved.

i) Which record appears first: 01.04.2012 or 04.11.2020?
- Both entries actually share field2 = 20.12.2022.
- The sort criteria are exhausted, so they remain in their original order:
○ Record with field4 = 01.04.2012 (originally row 3)
○ Record with field4 = 04.11.2020 (originally row 4)
-> 01.04.2012 appears before 04.11.2020.


ii) How does the date comparison work?
- Date fields (TYPE D) are stored internally in the format YYYYMMDD.
- Compared lexicographically/numerically For DESCENDING: 20221220 > 20201104 > 20130101 > 20120401

-> Newest dates end up at the top.
I hope my explanation has provided some clarity.

Best regards,
Dennis

entry.title: Re: Change company code from XYZ to ABC
entry.content:
Hi, 

there is no solution to change company code. You need to create all master data manually or using some development for new company code. 

Best regards, Łukasz

Any idea what is the reason? Is that because SAP maintenance? If yes, how can I easily check maintenance calendar plan by SAP for this trial account? Any link?

entry.title: Cost Distribution
entry.content:
Hi SAP Professionals,

I am currently facing an issue with cost distribution in SAP HCM. Despite accurate maintenance of Infotype 0027 (Cost Distribution), the system appears to disregard this data during the payroll posting run, resulting in no impact on the SAP payroll posting.

HCM (Human Capital Management)

entry.title: Intercompany Financial Posting 1GP - Different tax countries are not permitted
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are trying to post an intercompany financial transaction between two company codes (different countries) and we get this error message:

Different tax countries are not permitted

Taxes are always posted in the initial company code for cross-company-code transactions and stored in the tax information (BSET) of the initial company code. It has not been defined how taxes are subsequently handled when the countries of the company codes involved are different. An integrated company in the sales tax sense can no longer apply because the taxes relate to company codes for different countries.

System Response

Such transactions are not defined and are not permitted.

What is the point then of this test script if this isn't allowed? Why isn't this allowed? The error message is kind of blurry to me. We haven't included any taxes, the tax rate is 0% and the tax code used was V0. It works if the company codes are from the same country but unfortunately that's not useful in our case.

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Re: Patient Accounting Integration with Finance & Controlling
entry.content:
Hi ,

Currently I am also working on a SAP IS-H project and required the same document.

Could you please send the document to my email   ahmed elsmlawy1@gmail.com

Thanking you in advance for your help.

entry.title: Re: Workflow approval via Integration Center
entry.content:
Hi @mithilapethe,

In what scenario do you need to use integration center to approve workflow?

there are API queries that you can use to approve workflow though.

Regards,

Reza

entry.title: Re: 1H 2025 - SFSL-14795 - Redesigned Continuous Performa...
entry.content:
@BalajiNudurupat  With this new change of RBP the difference is 

Earlier  release we were having a single permission for continuous performance and another one for continuous feedback ,But with this change we can now control if we have to give only permission to view these functionalities or also permission to make changes for these functionalities ,That means in simple words we can control view permission and edit permission with dedicated permission with this release change.

Hope this clear .

Please mark this as Answer so other can get benefit of this.

Thanks

Mridul

entry.title: AP Invoices content repository
entry.content:
Hi,

 

i would like to know how can we access the content repository of AP invoices in SAP 2SL public cloud.

entry.title: Achieving Peak Performance of the Financial Close
entry.content:
We are happy to announce the availability of the SAP Cloud ERP Private, financial closing add-on to better help customers achieve ‘peak performance’ of their financial closing process.  The add-on also demonstrates the power and the flexibility of our partner ecosystem because the package includes SAP Account Substantiation and Automation by BlackLine, financial review option.  

This add-on has the following benefits for customers executing a financial close:

  • Run closing activities continuously on granular financial data before period-end for faster automated financial close
  • Ensure data consistency and integration including a ​complete audit trail
  • Eliminate inaccurate and repetitive intercompany closing processes with transactional-level intercompany matching and reconciliation​
  • facilitating unparalleled automation between accounting and corporate close
  • Import financial and non-financial data from multiple sources easily
  • Orchestrate and accelerate closing execution with collaboration workflows and full documentation to ensure compliance
  • Proactively analyze your financial statements with document variance commentary and account sign-off

Please see the Accounting and Financial Close | SAP Financial Management page for more product details and success stories.

entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors Variable Pay Forecasting Report
entry.content:
Dear all,

 

I am currently looking into the Forecasting Functionalities and have a few questions:

1. Is my understanding correct, that forecasting does not consider already existing timeslices. Eg. Bonus Year is Jan-Dec and I run a forecast in June. One employee is on Bonus Plan 1 with Business Goal A in January, and was moved to receive Bonus Plan 2 with Business Goal B as of February. Does the Forecast Export consider then the uploaded forecasting values for both entities? I tested this case, but the numbers on the export for that user were exactly the same in both cases (1 timeslice and 2 timeslices).

2. In several SAP notes is says that forecasting does not work for Templates with direct payout function type. What exactly is meant by this, because I have such a template, however, the system still provides the forecast results?

3. What is the purpose of the start and end date when setting up the bonus forecast? I set up 2, 1 from 01.Jan-31.Jan and one from 01.Jan-31.Dec. Both provided the exact same numbers.

4. In my template I didnt upload forecast amounts via Business Goals File. However, when I export the Forecasting Report I still see values in the column "Business Forecast 1 Percent". Why is this? Does this have to do with using a direct payout function template?

 

Thank you and best regards,

Rebecca

Rebecca

entry.title: Re: How to navigate from RFKK_MA_SCHEDULER to FQEVENTS
entry.content: Hi, RFKK_MA_SCHEDULER should be debugged like a background job via SM50. You  may also put a sample code with do..enddo loop with conditional exit

entry.title: Re: Creating a sales order with reference to an automatic purchase order via IDoc
entry.content: Good effort, thanks Mert.

entry.title: ME2O/ME2ON does not extracts Reservations for MRP Area
entry.content:
Dear colleagues,

As part of an SAP ECC to S/4HANA migration project for a multinational manufacturing company, we are implementing a reorganization of the Consignment Processing by adopting MRP Areas for vendors, replacing the previously used model.

In line with the guidelines provided by SAP Note 2516504 and this blog, we have configured the system to avoid the needing of creating specific material master data for the MRP Area and the automatic generation of transfer postings (movement type 541 O) from the Plant to the MRP Area to cover these requirements due to the MRP RUN (MD02, they don't use the MRP LIVE).

The planning process via MRP and the subsequent creation of transfer postings are functioning correctly. However, we have encountered unexpected behavior when using the ME2O (Stock provided to vendor) and ME2ON (Monitoring of stock provided to vendor) transactions.

Specifically, the ME2O/ME2ON transactions fail to extract the transfer postings if the components involved do not have specific material master data at the MRP Area level (i.e., there is no corresponding record in the MDMA table for the MRP Area in question). Consequently, it is impossible to use these transactions to initiate the shipment of components to the subcontractor.

This observed behavior seems to conflict with SAP guidelines, which would suggest that the creation of material master data at the MRP Area level is not a necessary prerequisite for the correct functioning of the scenario with MRP Areas for Consignment Processing.

Therefore, I would kindly like to request your support in understanding whether we have overlooked any fundamental configuration steps.

I'm available to provide further details on our configuration and the tests performed.

Thanks in advange

entry.title: change PO item short text when transferring shipment cost
entry.content:
shipment cost documents settles freight costs to purchase order. During this transfer, I need to programmatically change the short text of the PO item (EKPO-TXZ01) using ABAP, based on some logic derived from the shipment cost document (e.g., service type, carrier, etc.).

please advice proper badi, user exit, FM that should be used to achieve that.

 

 

ABAP Development #

entry.title: Re: Bem vindos a Comunidade SAP de Londrina!
entry.content:
ótima iniciativa, Bora para cima!

Sou aqui do interior do Paraná, espero poder ajudar nesse movimento.

Já atuo alguns anos no ecossistema SAP.

entry.title: Re: I created a new MM pricing condition that affects PB0...
entry.content:
Hi LuisCaFri,

to separate purchace price variance, or other kind of variances, in their nature you should use specific cost component in your material valuation and have an active actual costing and actual cost component split.

Without these one options is to determine the difference between gl account for material purchase, tariff costs by confronting it with the realeased standard cost estimate for the purchased material. 

Otherwise you could try to avoid debiting the purchased material with the new MM condition and register them in another CO object (i.e. cost center) to be able to allocate them to materials.

These solutions differs greatly and their effectivness rely on how the business model regarding inventory valuation is handled.

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content: Hi community! I am glad to join this group. It is great to have a place to like that and connect with others community members!

entry.title: Re: How run the Material ledger for FG & SFG with Price c...
entry.content:
Hi,

it should not possible to set multilevel price determination with price control V, outside specific scenarios such as split valuation.

It is not possible to use to run CKMLCP or AVR on materials that use transaction based price detemination.

And it is not adviced to use moving average for semifinished or finished goods. There are various KBA and sap notes in this regard (https://me.sap.com/notes/81682/E).

entry.title: Re: Valuation WIP in different ledgers w/o Material Ledger/Actual Costing
entry.content:
Hi,

What do you mean by "we need to post the material consumptions on the production order in different ledgers" ?

Without UPA material ledger costs are based on 0L.

But you can post differently the calculated WIP by try using different result analysis key and a updating the configuration in TCODE OKG8 to post in different accounting principles.

entry.title: Re: #SITREC2025 -🗣️Abertura do SAP Inside Track Recife
entry.content:
Sem sombra de dúvidas, um evento memorável! Informações e aprendizados incrivéis! Networking sensacional! O melhor evento da comunidade SAP no Brasil!

Phlavio

entry.title: Wrong password for new company database
entry.content:
Hi SAP B1 Experts,

I facing an issue with a newly created company DB. I'm unable to access the company since the system throws an error message "Incorrect password". Whatever password I enter, the system still reject it. I have checked all SAP services, B1Site user status and password are OK. I'm using SAP B1 v10 FP 2308.

Please can someone could give an idea on how to solve this issue?

Thanks for your support

Muledi

entry.title: Re: BSP future in Private Cloud 2023 FPS2
entry.content:
Yes, surprisingly there is no reference for BSP under Work Zone restrictions.
Source: Restrictions | SAP Help Portal

But before refactoring anything I suggest actually to review if such apps still make sense or can be replaced by standard apps. Something created in 2007 could have a standard solution in 2025. Do not only check S/4HANA common knowledge but also some hidden business functions.

If still make sense, you can evaluate if it's SAPUI5 freestyle, Fiori Elements, CAP, a mix of them or anything else based on the tools and services available.

entry.title: Re: Failed to get service instance from ADT
entry.content: Did you get any solution? I am facing the same problem.

entry.title: CS GoPay
entry.content: CS GoPay 081540_948738 hubungi Layanan Call Center GoPay di nomor resmi GoPay

entry.title: Re: Failed to get service instance from ADT
entry.content:
Hello,

There is upgrades planned for BTP ABAP Environment for 16 May 2025 and it seems even the Trial BTP ABAP Environment is also subjected for the upgrades.

Please find the BTP maintenance calendar plan schedule link:

What's New for SAP Business Technology Platform 

Hope this helps!

Thanks,

Sijin

entry.title: Re: CS GoPay
entry.content: Call Center GoPay

entry.title: Re: Failed to get service instance from ADT

SAP BTP ABAP Environment – Maintenance Windows and Major Upgrade Windows in 2025 

Thanks,

Sijin

entry.content:
Hello Santosh,

As discussed, here is more documentation related to adapting UI: 

Custom Fields App and Custom Logic App

Extend and Integrate Your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Key User Extensibility

SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition - Key User Extensibility (in-app extensibility) Examples

In-App Extensibility Overview (sap.com)
The last two links have very detailed information.

Thank you! 

Kind Regards,
Iza
